category_title: Students with inappropriate verbal behavior and off-task statements in academic contexts
minimal_context: Desi is sitting at his desk in a self-contained classroom working on independent textbook activities.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please open your textbook and begin the independent activity.

STUDENT: (Makes a loud buzzing noise) I'm not doing this stupid book.

TUTOR: You need to keep working on the assignment.

STUDENT: Why do I always have to do this? It's not fair and you're always making me do the worst stuff!

TUTOR: Just do your best and focus on the textbook.

STUDENT: Shut up! If you don't leave me alone I'm going to smash this tape recorder!

options.A: Emphasize mastery goals during independent textbook activities by valuing Desi's personal improvement and mastery of the task rather than grades or social comparison in the self-contained classroom.
options.B: Structure the independent textbook activities using cooperative learning as an alternative strategy for Desi, rather than having him work alone at his desk in the self-contained classroom.
options.C: Have the tape recorder sound a bell every minute, prompting Desi to record whether he had positive interactions during the preceding interval by checking Yes or No on a sheet of paper at his desk.
options.D: Provide Desi with a great deal of one-to-one instruction initially during the independent textbook activities, but gradually diminish this adult support over time in the self-contained classroom.
